sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIMESTAMP, duration INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, current_timestamp, INTERVAL '2 HOURS'), (2, current_timestamp - INTERVAL '30 MINUTES', INTERVAL '3 HOURS');
SELECT id, event_time + duration AS end_time FROM test_table WHERE event_time > current_timestamp - INTERVAL '1 HOUR';
SELECT id, event_time + INTERVAL (EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM current_timestamp) || ' MINUTES')::INTERVAL AS adjusted_time FROM test_table;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY event_time) AS occurrences FROM (SELECT id, event_time + INTERVAL '1 HOUR' AS adjusted_event_time FROM test_table) sub;
SELECT id, event_time, event_time + INTERVAL (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE id = t.id) HOUR AS future_time FROM test_table t;
SELECT id, CASE WHEN event_time IS NOT NULL THEN event_time + INTERVAL '1 HOUR' ELSE current_timestamp END AS safe_time FROM test_table;
SELECT current_timestamp AS now_time, (SELECT MAX(event_time) FROM test_table) + INTERVAL '1 DAY' AS max_time_plus_one_day;
SELECT id, event_time, (current_timestamp AT TIME ZONE 'UTC') + duration AS utc_end_time FROM test_table WHERE duration IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (current_timestamp + INTERVAL '30 MINUTES') AS in_30_mins, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE event_time < current_timestamp) AS count_before_now FROM test_table;
